text: Morning Beth — is Jack still hoping to come out to the Severn for the fishing morning in October? I want to hold the date.
He's counting the days! I can drop him at the dock around 7:30 if that works.
Perfect, 7:30 on the 10th. I'll have the rods and a thermos ready. And tell Emma I finished the book — Gettysburg is still on for the 24th.
Juan — Boatyard Tuesday at 11:30 as usual? Save us the booth by the window.
Wouldn't miss it, Lenny. I'll get there early and grab the booth.
Good man. I'll bring the Navy reunion flyer so we can pick a weekend.
Hi Juan, Patricia here — you left your reading glasses at the house Sunday. Want us to bring them Tuesday?
So that's where they went — yes please, Tuesday is perfect. Thank you, Patricia.
Done — they're in Lenny's coat pocket so we don't forget.
Beth, what time is Emma's school play on the 14th of November? I want to leave the afternoon open.
2:00 in the middle-school auditorium. I'm driving, so I can swing by and get you at 1:15.
1:15 it is. She's been practicing her lines with me on the phone — I wouldn't miss it.
Lenny, did the Naval Academy seawall reopen after the storm? Thinking of the morning walk tomorrow.
Open and clear. Meet you at the gate at 8?
8 o'clock, gate it is. See you there.
Beth — for the Gettysburg overnight with Emma on the 24th, is a Friday drop-off okay? I found a quiet inn near the battlefield.
Friday works great. She's already made a list of the sites she wants to see. Thank you for doing this, Juan.
My pleasure. I'll send the itinerary once I book the room. She can teach me a thing or two on the tour.
Morning Beth — is Jack still hoping for the Severn on the 10th? I want to hold the date.
He's counting the days!
Tell Emma I finished The Starling Effect. The ending got me.
The kids can't wait.
Thank you for driving to the play. I'll be in the third row with a proud look on my face.
Sounds perfect — thank you Juan.
The guest room is made up whenever the kids want a night here.
Emma says come at 2.
Jack's tackle box is ready. Don't tell him — let him find it Saturday.
You spoil them, and I love you for it.
Gettysburg is booked for the 24th. Emma can bring the sketchbook.
See you Sunday.
How did Jack's reading test go? He sounded nervous on the phone.
The soup won, obviously.
I made too much soup again. Send the kids over before it defeats me.
I'll have him at the dock by 7:30 sharp.
Photo from the feeder this morning — the cardinal Emma likes.
She hasn't stopped talking about the ending either.
Is Sunday still good for a visit? I'll have cocoa for the kids.
Third row it is — I'll wave.
Emma's next book pick came in at Old Fox. I'll grab two copies.
They love that guest room more than their own.
The fishing photos came out well. Sending three of the best.
Museum day is perfect if it rains.
Rain moved the yard plans — museum day instead if the kids are up for it?
Those photos are going straight on the fridge.
Give the kids a hug from their grandfather. Talk soon.
Talk Sunday — thank you, Juan.
Boatyard at 11:30 tomorrow? Corner table if I get there first.
Corner table it is.
Put the reunion on the calendar — Oct 2 to 4, Norfolk. Don't make me remind you again.
Calendar. Done. Happy now?
The Grant biography finally clicked around page 200. You were right.
Told you.
Can't do Tuesday — doctor moved me. Wednesday, same time?
Wednesday works.
Saw your name come up at the club after the Gazette letter. Well done.
Bring the book.
Coffee's on if you want to watch the game Saturday.
Game's on.
Ray wants a headcount for the reunion dinner. You in?
Count me in for the dinner.
Good lunch today. The soup was worth the wait as always.
Best soup on the creek.
Bring that book you mentioned — I'll trade you the Halberstam.
Deal — Halberstam for the Grant.
Weather looks fine for a seawall walk before lunch. Meet at the gate?
Meet you at the gate at ten.
Patricia — thank you for the casserole. Marion would have loved the recipe.
Any time, Juan.
The hydrangeas you recommended took beautifully in the front bed.
We'll be there Thursday.
Are you and Harold coming to the library talk Thursday?
Save two seats.
I saved you a seat at the historical society lecture.
You're always welcome, Juan.
Morning Beth — is Jack still hoping for the Severn on the 10th? I want to hold the date.
He's counting the days!
